title: Escalation Engineer Ladder Playbook
description: Build a cohesive support engineering ladder: entry support engineers and escalation engineers as one family. Counter stigma, give customers a credible next level, and teach when they want the wrong thing.

mindset.principle: Customers often want the wrong next level. Saying no makes them mad. An engineer who teaches makes them feel special and still steers the path.

mistakes.label: Only generic support title forever
mistakes.cost: Top talent leaves; customers assume all support sucks

mistakes.label: Title change with no skill or scope change
mistakes.cost: Credibility dies; eng laughs; customers not fooled long

mistakes.label: Tell customer they want the wrong thing with no teach
Escalation eng never feeds learning back to base support

ladder_rungs.label: Strong tech support with engineering posture and standards
Top notch; complex, multi-system, customer-critical next level
Same group culture; escalations teach the line below

decision_branches.situation: Need titles and scope for advanced support
Support brand is weak internally or externally

agent_signal.use_when: Need support engineer / escalation engineer career ladder. Customers demand next level. Support stigma. Teach path when they want the wrong thing.
decision_criteria: Titles match real scope and skill.
Escalation eng and support eng feed each other.
Wrong customer ask gets teach, not raw no.
agent_signal.operator_context: Field pattern: cohesive support structure; stigma counter; customer feels special with engineer teach.
agent_signal.not_for: Generic job spine only. Not HR legal advice on titles/pay.
trigger_phrases: Need support engineer / escalation engineer career ladder. Customers demand next
Need playbook: Escalation Engineer Ladder Playbook
